quotes.text: People ask how I pick the colors. I don't overthink it. If the dog looks a little too blue, well, maybe he was having a sad day. You just put it on the canvas and hope for the best.
quotes.speaker: George W. Bush
quotes.fake_note: Mirrors his folksy, unassuming attitude toward his post-presidency painting hobby. It perfectly matches his famously simple, literal sense of humor, though it's just a bit too on-the-nose for a real art critique.

quotes.text: I do not like broccoli. And I haven't liked it since I was a little kid, and my mother made me eat it. And I'm President of the United States, and I'm not going to eat any more broccoli.
quotes.speaker: George H. W. Bush
quotes.context: Bush's lighthearted declaration at his March 22, 1990 news conference, when asked whether he had 'lost the broccoli vote' after it emerged he'd banned broccoli from Air Force One. The remark became a running joke of his presidency and promp

source.title: George H.W. Bush: Hater of Broccoli - Smart Politics (University of Minnesota Libraries)

quotes.text: Where my reason, imagination or interest were not engaged, I would not or I could not learn.
quotes.speaker: Winston Churchill

source.title: My Early Life, by Winston S. Churchill (Project Gutenberg Canada full text)

quotes.text: I am a Ford, not a Lincoln. My addresses will never be as eloquent as Mr. Lincoln's. But I will do my very best to equal his brevity and his plain speaking.
quotes.speaker: Gerald Ford
quotes.context: Self-deprecating remark Ford made on December 6, 1973, immediately after taking the oath of office as the 40th Vice President (following Spiro Agnew's resignation). A pun on his own surname contrasting himself with Abraham Lincoln's eloquen

quotes.text: Look, I've accepted that my crossover is mostly just a suggestion at this point. The knees have formed their own filibuster.
quotes.speaker: Barack Obama
quotes.fake_note: Captures his deliberate, self-deprecating cadence and his well-documented love for basketball. The subtle giveaway is the slightly too-perfect integration of a legislative term (filibuster) to describe his aging joints.

quotes.text: I went into the coffee shop and they asked if I wanted oat milk, almond milk, or macadamia milk. I said, 'I want the milk that comes from a cow, please.' It shouldn't be this complicated.
quotes.speaker: Bernie Sanders
quotes.fake_note: Nails his signature curmudgeonly pragmatism and rhythmic 'It shouldn't be this complicated' phrasing. The giveaway is placing his classic anti-establishment frustration squarely onto trendy barista milk options.
